title: After Action Review — Fix Repeating Downs & Field Goals on Early Downs
scope: Fix play-by-play showing duplicate down & distance on consecutive plays, and prevent the coaching AI from kicking field goals on downs 1-3. 1 file changed, +48 / -3 lines

rationale.item: Incident: National Championship box score (Arizona State 72.0 vs Nebraska Omaha 74.5) showed multiple instances of "repeating downs" — consecutive plays displaying identical down, distance, and field position — plus a field goal kicked on what was effectively 3rd down.
Kicking a field goal with 4 downs remaining is irrational — you can always kick later if the drive stalls.

Four independent bugs combined to produce the observed symptoms: offensive during-play penalties silently consumed a down; defensive post-play penalties never granted automatic first downs; Play objects stored inconsistent pre-play vs post-play state; and select_play_family() maintained non-zero kick weights on all downs, bypassing the select_kick_decision() down 4-6 gate.
The bugs compounded: a single offensive holding call early in a drive could cascade into a lost down, a premature field goal, and a confusing box score — all from one penalty.

actions.description: Bug 1 fix (_apply_during_play_penalty, line ~5116): offensive during-play penalties now restore state.down and state.yards_to_go to pre-play values saved at the start of simulate_play(), so the down replays instead of being silently consumed.

Bug 2 fix (_apply_post_play_penalty, line ~5155): defensive post-play penalties now award an automatic first down when penalty.yards >= yards_to_go (down = 1, yards_to_go = 20), matching the checks already present in _apply_pre_snap_penalty and during-play defensive handling.

Bug 3 fix: simulate_play() now saves _pre_play_fp/_pre_play_down/_pre_play_ytg before _simulate_play_core() and stamps them on every returned Play, so every play consistently shows the starting situation; this also implicitly fixes EPA's ep_before calculation.
Bug 4 fix (select_play_family, line ~5598): zeroed out field_goal, snap_kick, and punt weights on downs 1-3, with an exception for desperation clock situations (Q2/Q4 under 45 seconds).
Commit d8070a6 "Fix repeating downs in play-by-play and field goals on early downs".

risk_posture: Snap kicks on downs 2-3 are still possible through _check_snap_kick_shot_play(), which is a controlled decision (kicker skill + coaching style check), not a random weight selection. The desperation exception allows last-second kicks when there literally isn't time for another play.
Drive summary stats: Drive yards are accumulated from play.yards_gained (unchanged), not from field position deltas, so the fix doesn't affect drive totals.

non_actions.reason: Already correctly replayed the down (offensive) and checked for auto-first-down (defensive)
Already checked for auto-first-down at line 5127-5129
Opportunistic snap kicks on downs 2-3 still work; only the uncontrolled weight-based selection was gated
Now implicitly fixed: p.field_position and p.down are pre-play values, so calculate_ep(p.field_position, p.down) correctly computes ep_before
Drive yards are accumulated from play.yards_gained (unchanged), not from field position deltas, so the fix doesn't affect drive totals

section_headers_original: After Action Review — Fix Repeating Downs & Field Goals on Early Downs
Bug 1 — During-Play Offensive Penalties Don't Replay the Down
Bug 2 — Post-Play Defensive Penalties Don't Grant Automatic First Down
Bug 3 — Play Objects Store Inconsistent State (Pre-Play vs Post-Play)
Bug 4 — Field Goals and Snap Kicks Selectable on Downs 1-3
unmapped_content:
Branch: claude/fix-championship-game-logic-zoWVE.

Incident play-by-play examples:
17:24  UNO  55  2&17  speed option  3
17:07  UNO  55  2&17  KICK PASS    45  — TOUCHDOWN!
50  3&5  sweep option   3
50  3&5  SPEED OPTION  50  — TOUCHDOWN!
42  2&13  PENALTY  7   — draw 7 + Unsportsmanlike on D 15 yds
42  2&13  KICK PASS 58 — TOUCHDOWN!
44:55  ASU  86  2&17  FIELD GOAL  15  — 39yd FG GOOD!

Bug 1 mechanism detail: a 3-yard gain on 1st & 20 followed by holding left state at 2nd & 17 at FP 28 instead of replaying 1st & 20; over a full game this could cost a team 2-4 downs.

Bug 3 mechanism detail: GAIN/FIRST_DOWN plays store post-play values; TOUCHDOWN/scoring plays store pre-play values, so a GAIN followed by a TD both display the same down/FP/ytg. Secondary impact: EPA used post-play state for ep_before on GAIN plays.

Bug 4 code path: simulate_play() → _simulate_play_core() → select_play_family() (can return FIELD_GOAL on down 2) → PLAY_FAMILY_TO_TYPE[FIELD_GOAL] = PLACE_KICK → _dispatch_play(PLACE_KICK) → simulate_place_kick(). The select_kick_decision() gate is never consulted.

Interaction Between Bugs: 1) Bug 1 (penalty steals a down) → offense is on 2nd down when it should be 1st; 2) Bug 4 (FG on early downs) → weighted selection picks field_goal on that 2nd down; 3) Bug 3 (inconsistent state) → play-by-play shows "2&17" twice in a row; 4) Bug 2 (no auto-first-down) → defensive personal foul doesn't reset the down, perpetuating the off-by-one.

Design Note: The _pre_play_* attributes serve dual purpose: display consistency and penalty rollback. This avoids needing to reverse-engineer the pre-play state from the post-play state (which would require knowing exactly which code path modified state and how).
parser_notes: Same branch and same incident game as docs/aar_2026-03-16_championship_kick_decision_logic.md — companion AAR. No validation/testing section.
